response:
Here is complete pytest-style test suite with full coverage for the given function, following best practices:
```python
import pytest

# Import your function here, replace with actual module location
def snake_to_camel(s: str) -> str:
    parts = s.split('_')
    return parts[0] + ''.join(p.capitalize() for p in parts[1:])


class TestSnakeToCamel:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "input_str, expected_output",
        [
            # ✅ Happy Path Normal Cases
            ("hello", "hello"),
            ("hello_world", "helloWorld"),
            ("full_snake_case_sentence", "fullSnakeCaseSentence"),
            ("user_id_456", "userId456"),
            ("api_v3_endpoint", "apiV3Endpoint"),

            # 🚩 Edge Cases
            ("", ""),                      # Empty string input
            ("x", "x"),                    # Single character input
            ("a_b", "aB"),                 # Minimal 2-part snake case
            ("_internal_var", "InternalVar"), # Leading underscore
            ("ends_with_underscore_", "endsWithUnderscore"), # Trailing underscore
            ("double__underscore", "doubleUnderscore"), # Consecutive underscores
            ("___", ""),                   # Only underscores
            ("alreadyCamelCase", "alreadyCamelCase"), # Already camel input
            ("mixed_CASE_input", "mixedCaseInput"), # Mixed case input parts
        ],
    )
    def test_valid_input_behaviour(self, input_str, expected_output):
        """Test all valid inputs produce expected output for this implementation"""
        assert snake_to_camel(input_str) == expected_output

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "invalid_input",
        [
            None, 123, 78.9, True,
            [], {}, ("tuple",), b'bytes_value', object()
        ],
    )
    def test_non_string_inputs_raise(self, invalid_input):
        """Verify invalid types correctly throw exceptions"""
        with pytest.raises((AttributeError, TypeError)):
            snake_to_camel(invalid_input)

    def test_none_input_explicitly(self):
        """Common error case: explicit test for None input"""
        with pytest.raises(AttributeError, match="'NoneType' object has no attribute 'split'"):
            snake_to_camel(None)
```

---

### Coverage Breakdown:
| Category | Coverage Details |
|---|---|
| **Happy Path** | Standard snake case inputs, multi-part names, values with numbers |
| **Edge Cases** | Empty string, single characters, leading/trailing/consecutive underscores, already camel cased input, all-underscore input |
| **Error Cases** | All common invalid types, explicit test for the extremely common `None` bad input |

> 💡 Important Note:
> These tests match **the actual behaviour of the provided implementation**. This original function has known behaviour that may not match ideal snake case standards:
> 1.  It will discard leading underscores
> 2.  It will collapse consecutive underscores
> 3.  It will silently remove trailing underscores
>
> If these behaviours are unintended bugs, modify the `snake_to_camel` function first, then update test expectations.
